name: Isolate Diffuse Nebular Emission via Point Source Suppression
description:
Astronomers often need to separate stars from background nebular gas. Apply a morphological separation technique (a spatial Median filter) to the HST H-alpha image of the Eagle Nebula (~/AstroImages/nebula_analysis/eagle_ha_656nmos.fits) using AstroImageJ to erase the stars while preserving the nebula's structure. Save the resulting starless image to ~/AstroImages/nebula_analysis/starless_ha.fits. Measure the maximum pixel intensity of the original image (representing the brightest star core) and the maximum pixel intensity of the filtered image (representing the brightest nebular gas). Create a text file at ~/AstroImages/nebula_analysis/suppression_stats.txt containing exactly these three lines:
Original_Max: [value]
Filtered_Max: [value]
Median_Radius_Used: [value]
Use the AstroImageJ GUI for all processing (Login: ga / password123). Do not use external scripts.
